page: Microsoft Azure D96alds_v7 is a compute-focused virtual machine powered by an AMD EPYC 9V45 processor. Operating on the x86_64 architecture under Microsoft Hyper-V, this instance provides 96 dedicated vCPUs mapped from 48 physical cores with multithreading enabled. The CPU features a base frequency of 4.5 GHz, supported by 48 KB L1d, 1.0 MB L2, and 32 MB L3 cache. It is equipped with 192.0 GB of RAM, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB per vCPU, and includes 5669 GB of local storage.

Benchmark results place this server in the top tier for both single-core and multi-core CPU performance via stress-ng, as well as Passmark CPU tests. The AMD EPYC processor's instruction set, including AVX-512 and cryptography extensions, drives top-tier performance in encryption, compression, and physics simulations. Memory bandwidth is highly competitive, registering top-tier speeds for small-block cached reads and large-block writes, alongside strong large-block uncached reads.

With a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, the instance trades massive memory capacity for dense, high-frequency compute power. This configuration is highly cost-efficient for compute-bound workloads that do not require massive memory footprints but benefit from high-speed local storage. It is optimized for high-throughput database operations, static web serving, Redis deployments, and CPU-based LLM inference, where it achieves top-tier text generation speeds.
description: Microsoft Azure D96alds_v7 is a compute-optimized virtual machine featuring 96 dedicated vCPUs and 48 physical cores powered by an AMD EPYC 9V45 processor running at 4.5 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture under Microsoft Hyper-V, it provides 192.0 GB of RAM and 5669 GB of local storage. The server delivers top-tier performance in multi-core processing, cryptography, and database operations, supported by advanced instruction sets like AVX-512. While its 1:2 memory-to-vCPU ratio represents a tradeoff for memory-intensive applications, its high-frequency compute and massive local disk capacity make it highly cost-efficient for compute-heavy workloads. It is best suited for high-throughput web serving, database operations, Redis deployments, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D96alds_v7 is a compute-optimized virtual machine featuring 96 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs, 192 GB of RAM, and 5669 GB of local storage, delivering top-tier processing and database performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D96alds_v7 is a compute-optimized VM with 96 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 192 GB RAM, and 5669 GB local storage for high-throughput workloads.
tagline: A high-frequency 96-vCPU compute instance with dense local storage, optimized for demanding database operations and parallel processing workloads.
bullet_points: 96 dedicated vCPUs powered by a 4.5 GHz AMD EPYC 9V45 processor
192 GB of system memory and 5669 GB of high-capacity local storage
Top-tier multi-core CPU, cryptography, and database benchmark performance
Advanced instruction support including AVX-512 and vector extensions
Optimized for high-throughput web serving, Redis, and CPU-based LLM inference
